seo.title: AI Managed Services for Legal Teams | AI Workflow Automation
seo.description: AI managed services and workflow automation consulting for law firms. Dodonai designs, builds, and runs custom AI agents around your workflows. Blueprint in 1-2 weeks. SOC 2 + HIPAA.

hero.headline: AI Managed Services for Legal Teams
hero.subheadline: You know your firm needs AI. You don't know where to start. Dodonai is the AI workflow automation and consulting partner for law firms — we design, build, and run custom agents around the workflows your team already uses, with SOC 2 and HIPAA guardrails built in. Launch in weeks.
bullets: Win back the hours lost to repetitive work
Custom agents live in weeks, not quarters
Built around the tools and workflows you already use

howItWorks.title: Blueprint
howItWorks.description: We sit with your team, map your highest-leverage workflows, and audit the tools you already use (practice management, email, calendar, document management). You walk away with a written plan that shows exactly which agents to build, in what order, and what each one is expected to save you.

howItWorks.title: Build
howItWorks.description: We deploy 3 to 6 custom agents tuned to your firm. Intake triage, {{link:/deposition-summary-software/|deposition prep}}, deadline monitors, {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|medical chronologies}}, client update digests, whichever ones save the most attorney hours. Integrations, training, and documentation are all included, and the agents live in your account.

howItWorks.title: Managed
howItWorks.description: We keep the agents running, add new ones as your practice changes, and share what we learn from other firms we work with. Monthly reviews, priority support, and continuous improvement on a simple retainer.

cards.title: Full implementation partner
cards.description: We sit with your team, understand the work, and deliver agents that fit how your firm actually operates. We stay involved through launch and into daily use.

cards.title: AI-model agnostic
cards.description: We use the best available model for each job and update as the frontier moves, so you benefit from every new release without switching vendors.

cards.title: Safety-first architecture
cards.description: Agents create drafts. They never send, file, or commit anything without your review. Every action is logged, and the attorney stays in the pilot seat.

cards.title: We optimize your AI spend
cards.description: New models ship constantly, and so does the cheapest way to run any given task well. We continuously re-architect your agents onto the best price-to-performance option as the frontier moves — because law-firm margins are paramount, and we treat your model spend like our own.

proof.caption: Average results across Dodonai engagements. Your numbers depend on firm size, practice area, and which agents you turn on.

positioning.heading: The missing middle for small-firm AI
positioning.intro: Most AI help for smaller firms comes in three flavors: a course that teaches your team to prompt and leaves the building to you, another subscription app you have to learn and run yourself, or an embedded consultant priced for AmLaw 100 budgets. We're the part in between. We build the agents around how your firm already works and hand them over, scoped to a solo or small-firm budget.

cards.title: Not another course
cards.description: Training programs teach your team to prompt better, then leave the implementation to you. We do the building. You get working agents wired into your stack, not a reading list.

cards.title: Not a generic app
cards.description: Off-the-shelf legal AI makes you bend your practice to its workflow. We build around your templates, your matter states, and the tools you already use, and everything lives in your account.

cards.title: Not big-firm consulting
cards.description: The embedded AI consultants serving law firms are built for 500-lawyer shops at $200k-plus engagements. Our builds start in the low four figures, sized for solo and small firms.

positioning.closer: And it's an architecture we run on ourselves every day, so the playbook your firm gets is one that's already in production, not a pitch deck.

faq.question: How long does a typical engagement take?
faq.answer: A Blueprint takes 1 to 2 weeks. A Build can run anywhere from a week to several months depending on scope — a single agent is fast, while rewiring a whole firm is a bigger lift. Managed support is ongoing on a monthly cadence.

faq.question: Who owns the agents you build?
faq.answer: You do. The skills, configurations, and prompts live in your account. If we ever part ways, your agents keep running and your team keeps working.

faq.question: What happens if the approach doesn't work for us?
faq.answer: We scope outcomes up front for every agent and adjust until each one is doing its job. If the Blueprint phase doesn't leave your firm with a clear path forward, we refund it. We'd rather walk away than push a bad fit.

faq.question: How do you handle our data?
faq.answer: Agents run inside infrastructure you control. Our LLM partners operate under zero data retention, so nothing is stored after processing. No client data is used to train models. SOC 2 and HIPAA controls apply to every engagement, and we'll sign a BAA where appropriate. More detail on the {{link:/ai-managed-services/safety/|safety page}}.

faq.question: How does this relate to the Dodonai app?
faq.answer: The {{link:/|Dodonai app}} is a great starting point for firms who want document AI right away, things like deposition summaries, medical chronologies, and extract-and-draft agents. AI Managed Services is for teams who want custom workflows that go beyond a single tool. Many firms use both, and we often use the app as a foundation inside a services engagement.

faq.question: Do we need a Claude Pro or Max subscription?
faq.answer: For most managed engagements, yes. You pay Anthropic directly for the Claude subscription (typically $20 to $100 per seat each month), and we build and run the agents that use it. We're AI-model agnostic though, so if your firm standardizes on a different provider we can work with that too.

faq.question: Can we start small?
faq.answer: Yes. Most firms start with a Blueprint, pick 1 or 2 agents for a first Build cycle, and scale from there. You can stop at any stage, and you keep everything we've built for you.

faq.question: How do we get started?
faq.answer: Email hello@dodon.ai or {{link:https://calendly.com/nick-dodonai|book an intro call}}. We want to understand your workflows and your goals before we talk scope or timing.
